purpose: Deterministic wordlist for text that ENTERS model context (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions, manifests, dispatch prompts, issue templates). Pinned/LOCKED custody artifacts are OUT OF SCOPE — canonical terminology stays canonical in canon. This lexicon prevents ordinary CI/CD automation prompts from pattern-matching the 'frontier LLM development' safeguard category.
scope_rule: Apply to context-entering surfaces only. Never edit blob-SHA-pinned artifacts (CUSTODY_AUTHORITY_PINS). P7 terminology law governs canon; this lexicon governs prompts.
classifier_categories_defended_against: frontier LLM development (distributed training infrastructure, ML accelerator design, kernel development)

terms.why: reads as recursive AI self-improvement; yours is a kaizen backlog applying scoped fixes from failure receipts
'kernel development' is a verbatim category phrase; 'motor kernel' pattern-matches it
'ML accelerator design' is a verbatim category phrase
'autonomous' + execution vocabulary stacks toward the category; 'scheduled' is honest and benign
self-* verbs stack with other signals
self-* verbs stack with other signals
fleet-of-workers phrasing reads as training infrastructure
reads as ML-system component; it is a job that may call an API
infrastructure-plane vocabulary stacks toward training-infra reading
novel machinery nouns invite worst-case reading; 'pipeline' is universally understood CI vocabulary
benign alone; flag only when file also has MEDIUM/HIGH hits

principle: Describe tasks as what they concretely are: workflows, cron jobs, scripts, PRs, receipts. Same system, plain CI/CD nouns. Truth lives in receipts; prompts can afford ordinary language.
